3100	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover (gap1, P1)]
3101	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}
3102	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen}
3103	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib, lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib,
3104	    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib, lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib,
3105	    lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib, lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}
3106	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen does NOT yet exist; it is the
3107	  % per-element transport step of gap1. It replaces the former lem:exists_isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_cover,
3108	  % whose \lean{} pin named a non-existent decl (and whose Mayer--Vietoris framing is superseded by
3109	  % the section-localization descent of lem:section_localization_descent).
3110	  \textit{Per-element step: on each basic open of the refined cover, the restricted sheaf is a
3111	  geometric tilde.}
3112	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) with quasi-coherence data \(q\), and let \(r \in R\)
3113	  be such that \(D(r) \le q.X_i\) for some member of the cover (as produced by
3114	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). Then the restricted counit is an
3115	  isomorphism,
3116	  \[
3117	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr),
3118	  \]
3119	  i.e. \(M|_{D(r)}\) is identified with the tilde \(\widetilde{N}\) of the \(R_r\)-module
3120	  \(N = \Gamma(M, D(r))\) on \(\Spec R_r \cong D(r)\).
3121	\end{lemma}
3122	\begin{proof}
3123	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib, lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib,
3124	    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib, lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib,
3125	    lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
3126	  The quasi-coherence datum supplies a presentation
3127	  \(q.\mathrm{presentation}\,i : (M.\mathrm{over}\,(q.X_i)).\mathrm{Presentation}\) on the slice over
3128	  \(q.X_i\). Restrict it along the inclusion of opens \(D(r) \subseteq q.X_i\) by transporting across
3129	  the iterated Grothendieck slice — the template is \(\mathrm{QuasicoherentData.bind}\)
3130	  (\cref{lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}) — using \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
3131	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the colimit-preserving slice functor; this yields a
3132	  presentation of \(M.\mathrm{over}\,(D(r))\). Pushing through the slice-to-geometric bridge
3133	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso} converts it into a presentation of the geometric restriction
3134	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,(D(r)).\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\); then, across the affine
3135	  identification \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, by
3136	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\) along the pullback functor \cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}), into
3137	  a genuine global presentation of \(M|_{D(r)}\) as a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R_r\). A global
3138	  presentation forces the \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-counit to be an isomorphism
3139	  (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib}), giving
3140	  \(\mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr)\).
3141	\end{proof}

3145	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D)]
3146	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent}
3147	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}
3148	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3149	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3150	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3151	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3152	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent does NOT yet exist; it is the
3153	  % genuine new content of gap1 and is reused by gap2's section-localization characterization.
3154	  % isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation does NOT close it: that lemma needs a GLOBAL
3155	  % presentation of M, whereas here only per-D(r) tilde data is available.
3156	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S "Sections over principal opens",
3157	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2152-L2170).
3158	  % Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.3.
3159	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3160	  % "Let $X$ be a scheme. Let $f \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
3161	  %  Denote $X_f \subset X$ the open where $f$ is invertible, see
3162	  %  Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-f-open}.
3163	  %  If $X$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated, the canonical map
3164	  %  $$
3165	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{O}_X)
3166	  %  $$
3167	  %  is an isomorphism. Moreover, if $\mathcal{F}$ is a quasi-coherent
3168	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules the map
3169	  %  $$
3170	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})
3171	  %  $$
3172	  %  is an isomorphism."
3173	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
3174	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
3175	  Let \(M\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) (equivalently, by
3176	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} and
3177	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}, \(M\) is locally isomorphic to a
3178	  tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) on the members of a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) of
3179	  \(\Spec R\)). Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3180	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localized module
3181	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); that is, it is
3182	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
3183	\end{lemma}
3184	% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
3185	% below is a project-internal descent (finite sheaf equalizer over the cover + flatness of
3186	% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is
3187	% gap1 itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.
3188	\begin{proof}
3189	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3190	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3191	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3192	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3193	  Refine the quasi-coherence cover to a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}_{j=1}^m\) of \(\Spec R\)
3194	  with each \(D(r_j) \le q.X_{i(j)}\)
3195	  (\cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). On each \(D(r_j)\) the restricted
3196	  counit is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), so
3197	  \(M|_{D(r_j)} \cong \widetilde{N_j}\) with \(N_j = \Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) a module over \(R_{r_j}\),
3198	  and the basic-open sections of \(M\) below \(D(r_j)\) are the corresponding module localizations
3199	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}).
3200	
3201	  Fix \(f \in R\). The sheaf condition for \(M\) over the finite cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) and its pairwise
3202	  intersections presents the global sections as the finite equalizer
3203	  \[
3204	    \Gamma(M, \top) \;=\; \mathrm{eq}\Bigl(\textstyle\prod_j \Gamma(M, D(r_j))
3205	      \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(M, D(r_jr_k))\Bigr),
3206	  \]
3207	  the Mayer--Vietoris / unique-gluing description of sections
3208	  (\cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}, \cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}); the same holds
3209	  with \(\top\) replaced by \(D(f)\) and the cover by \(\{D(fr_j)\}\). Localizing at
3210	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) is exact and commutes with this finite limit because localization is flat
3211	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}). On each cover piece the localization of
3212	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) away from \(f\) is \(\Gamma(M, D(fr_j)) = \Gamma(M|_{D(r_j)}, D(f))\), a
3213	  localization of the tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) computed by
3214	  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}; the same on the overlaps. Hence the localized equalizer
3215	  computes \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\), and the comparison map
3216	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is an isomorphism: the section
3217	  restriction is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\). This is the
3218	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})\) of Stacks
3219	  \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} on \(X = \Spec R\), \(X_f = D(f)\), obtained directly from
3220	  local-tilde data without appeal to the global affine equivalence.
3221	\end{proof}

2417	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(S \subseteq R\) be a multiplicative
2418	  submonoid, and let \(R_S = S^{-1}R\) be the localization of \(R\) at \(S\). Let
2419	  \(M\) be a \emph{finitely generated} \(R\)-module and let \(f : M \to M_S\) be
2420	  an \(R\)-linear map exhibiting \(M_S\) as the localized module \(S^{-1}M\)
2421	  (i.e.\ \(f\) is a localization of \(M\) at \(S\)). Then the annihilator of
2422	  \(M_S\) over \(R_S\) is the extension of the annihilator of \(M\) along the
2423	  structure map \(R \to R_S\):
2424	  \[
2425	    \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)
2426	    \;=\; (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S
2427	    \;=\; \mathrm{map}\bigl(R \to R_S\bigr)\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}_R M\bigr).
2428	  \]
2429	  Equivalently, for finitely generated \(M\) the annihilator commutes with
2430	  localization: \(\mathrm{Ann}(S^{-1}M) = S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}(M)\).
2431	
2432	  \emph{The finite-generation hypothesis is essential.} For a module that is not
2433	  finitely generated the inclusion \(S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}(M) \subseteq
2434	  \mathrm{Ann}(S^{-1}M)\) can be strict, so the identity fails. This is the
2435	  standard commutative-algebra fact that the annihilator of a finite module
2436	  localizes; it is recorded here as the load-bearing algebra engine for the
2437	  basic-open coherence of \cref{def:modules_annihilator}.
2438	\end{lemma}
2439	
2440	\begin{proof}
2441	  \(\supseteq\): if \(r \in \mathrm{Ann}_R M\), then \(r\) kills every element of
2442	  \(M\), hence its image \(r/1 \in R_S\) kills every element of \(M_S\) (each such
2443	  element is \(m/s = (1/s)\cdot f(m)\) for some \(m \in M\), \(s \in S\), and
2444	  \((r/1)\cdot(m/s) = (1/s)\cdot f(r\,m) = 0\)). Thus
2445	  \((\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\).
2446	
2447	  \(\subseteq\): write \(M = \langle m_1, \dots, m_n\rangle\) using the
2448	  finite-generation hypothesis, and let \(y \in \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\). Write
2449	  \(y = a/s\) with \(a \in R\), \(s \in S\). For each generator \(m_i\) the element
2450	  \(y\cdot f(m_i) = (a/s)\cdot f(m_i) = 0\) in \(M_S\); by the definition of the
2451	  localized module this means there is \(u_i \in S\) with \(u_i\,a\,m_i = 0\) in
2452	  \(M\). Put \(u = \prod_{i=1}^n u_i \in S\). Then \(u\,a\) kills every generator
2453	  \(m_i\), hence kills all of \(M\), so \(u\,a \in \mathrm{Ann}_R M\). Finally
2454	  \(y = a/s = (u\,a)/(u\,s)\) lies in \(S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}_R M =
2455	  (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S\). Therefore \(\mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S) \subseteq
2456	  (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S\), and combined with the reverse inclusion the two
2457	  ideals coincide. The use of a single common multiplier \(u = \prod_i u_i\) is
2458	  exactly the step that requires finitely many generators.
2459	\end{proof}

theorem genericFlatnessAlgebraic
    (A B M : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
  by_cases hAM : Module.Finite A M
  · -- Primary route: `M` module-finite over `A` ⟹ the finite-module helper.
    haveI := hAM
    exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
  · -- Surviving residue: `M` finite over the finite-type algebra `B` but not
    -- module-finite over `A`. Dévissage over the noetherian ring `B`
    -- (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`) with motive
    -- `P N := ∃ f ≠ 0, Free A_f (N_f)`, each `B`-module `N` carrying its restricted
    -- `A`-action through `algebraMap A B`. The subsingleton obligation discharges
    -- from the torsion base case (L1); the short-exact obligation from the L3
    -- splice; the `N ≅ B/𝔭` obligation is the genuine residual (it needs L4
    -- finiteness + L5, the polynomial-ring core still under construction).
    haveI : IsNoetherianRing B := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing A B

      refine IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime B
        (inferInstance : Module.Finite B M)
